repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3.1 only records Manski bounded-outcome wrappers, not spell-history Lipschitz extension

published_prior_art.cite: McShane1934/Whitney1934: classical real-valued Lipschitz extension envelopes; KimKwonKwonLee2018: smoothness tightens counterfactual-outcome bounds

repo_prior_art.cite: closest banked entries pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v3, pid_long_term_surrogate_v1, and pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 use IV memory, surrogate drift, and DID anticipation boxes, not reversible spell-history metrics

published_prior_art.cite: KimKwonKwonLee2018 gives smooth treatment-response partial-ID bounds; DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 gives generic finite-discrete sharp or epsilon-sharp bounds

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue reversible-spell finite-radius tightening functional Gamma_T(P;rho)

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiPepper2018 and KimKwonKwonLee2018 already show bounded-variation/smoothness restrictions tighten partial-ID bounds; DiemerShiSwanson2024 supplies the unrestricted dynamic pole
Han2021 studies nonparametric identification of finite dynamic treatment effects; the Cesaro statement is a conditional limit corollary of Conjecture 1 rather than a separate regime-opening result

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: DiemerShiSwanson2024 is listed as Epidemiology 35(4):495--503, but verified sources give Epidemiology 35(3):308--312, May 2024.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The two-terminal envelope formula is already classical McShane-Whitney Lipschitz-extension algebra; the proposal admits this but it should not count toward novelty.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The comparison to KimKwonKwonLee2018 understates how much of the kernel is obtained by treating finite treatment histories as the treatment domain; absent nontrivial cross-time/pathwise compatibility constraints, the dynamic lift is incremental.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the open-set strict width gap is a useful dynamic sensitivity result, but as written it is a routine finite-radius Lipschitz tightening unless tied to a published open regime or a genuinely new compatibility obstruction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make the flagship compatibility claim genuinely dynamic by adding a pathwise or cross-time restriction that prevents independent period-by-period pasting, then state the exact new obstruction or construction needed for endpoint laws.
Revise the KimKwonKwonLee2018 comparison to concede that history vectors can be viewed as a treatment space, then isolate what their smooth-response framework cannot express mathematically.
Fix the DiemerShiSwanson2024 bibliography metadata and add DOI/page verification for all checklist papers in §13.

To meet the flagship floor, add a strict-extension claim citing a published open problem: identify a larger reversible/nonstationary class explicitly left unresolved by an existing dynamic partial-ID or panel-bounds paper.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a nontrivial dynamic compatibility or published-open-regime extension; otherwise downgrade the target tier to field.
